Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log in a DuckDB table event log with columns case id activity timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For each case id we need to order the activities by timestamp and then aggregate them into a sequence that represents the variant Then we group by this sequence to get the count of cases for each variant We then determine the top K variants by frequency count of cases We then filter the original event log to only include cases that belong to these top K variants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Approach for step 1 We can use string agg with an order by inside to create a string of activities for each case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively we can use array agg and then order but note that the order in an array is not guaranteed unless we specify it

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note We are in DuckDB DuckDB supports string agg with an order by clause

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we can use this subquery to count variants Step 2 Count the variants and get the top K Step 3 Then we join back to the original table to get the events for the cases that are in the top K variants

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note We are to return all events from the original event log for cases that are in the top K variants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can do select event log all from event log where case id in select case id from case variants where variant in select variant from top variants

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But wait we have the variant in the case variants and we have the top variants We can also do select event log all from event log inner join case variants on event log case id equals case variants case id inner join top variants on case variants variant equals top variants variant

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: This should be more efficient

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But note The same case id appears multiple times in event log We are joining on case id so we will get all the events for that case

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Alternatively we can avoid the double join by first getting the case ids from the top variants and then joining with event log

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Another approach with case variants top variant cases and then select event log all from event log inner join top variant cases on event log case id equals top variant cases case id

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: This might be more efficient because we are not joining on a string variant but on an integer case id However note that the string comparison might be heavy

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can do with case variants as select case id string agg activity order by timestamp as variant from event log group by case id top variants as select variant from case variants group by variant order by count all desc limit 10 top variant cases as select case id from case variants where variant in select variant from top variants select event log all from event log inner join top variant cases on event log case id equals top variant cases case id

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: But wait we are only asked for the top K variants We are using 10 as an example We can make it a parameter

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We are returning all the original columns for the events that belong to the top K variants

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We might want to order by case id and timestamp to have a natural order

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: The problem does not specify tie breaking We can leave it as is which means that if there are ties the result is non deterministic Alternatively we can break ties by the variant string alphabetical to have a deterministic result
